commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
Adds an experimental built-in Apple Virtualization.framework provider, native helper packaging, lifecycle implementation, tests, CI/release changes, configuration, and documentation for local ARM64 Linux VMs on Apple Silicon.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR adds a new provider rather than fixing established behavior. Its workflow is directly testable on Apple Silicon, and the contributor supplied detailed final-head live output.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The concrete automated repair is limited to removing two release-owned changelog additions; the remaining ownership and merge-risk decision belongs to maintainers.

target.title: feat(providers): add experimental apple-vz provider for local Linux VMs
target.body:
Fixes https://github.com/openclaw/crabbox/issues/262.

## Summary

Adds the built-in `apple-vz` provider for full ARM64 Ubuntu VMs on Apple Silicon Macs running macOS 13 or newer.

Apple VZ is direct and local-only. It uses Apple `Virtualization.framework`, exposes guest SSH through a loopback VSOCK proxy, and plugs into the normal Crabbox SSH-lease workflow:

- `warmup`
- `run`
- `ssh`
- `status`
- `list`
- `stop`
- `cleanup`

## What ships

- Provider registration, config, flags, defaults, and Ubuntu ARM64 image mapping.
- Native `crabbox-apple-vz-helper` packaged beside the CLI in GoReleaser archives and Homebrew installs.
- Verified image download and cache, bounded QCOW2 conversion, sparse per-lease disks, NoCloud seed generation, EFI boot, NAT, VSOCK SSH, and retained leases.
- Content-addressed managed helper installation with ad-hoc signing, required entitlements, integrity digests, use locks, and safe old-version cleanup.
- Full provider docs, configuration reference, troubleshooting, security model, limits, and live-smoke support.

## Hardening

Pre-merge review expanded this substantially beyond the original implementation:

- Atomic, checksum-keyed image caches with cancellation, size limits, redirect policy, local-file identity, backing-file rejection, and interrupted-preparation recovery.
- Rollback and diagnostics for every startup phase; metadata-less cleanup; stale-start recovery; bounded helper termination.
- Collision-resistant Darwin kernel process identity before signaling, plus verified migration of older `ps` identities.
- Active image preparation is visible as `starting`; SSH fields remain empty until a real endpoint exists.
- Secret-safe signed image URL handling through stdin; remote images require HTTPS and SHA-256.
- Strict CPU, memory, disk, user, path, helper, host architecture, and macOS-version validation.
- Retained leases remain controllable when the configured source helper moves or disappears.
- Guest-initiated reverse VSOCK channels avoid uncancellable host connection attempts; channel counts, activation, stale replacement, and service-restart recovery are bounded and tested.
- Corrupt QCOW2 images, non-regular local image paths, and blocking FIFO paths are rejected before VM startup.

## Verification

Local CI-equivalent gate:

- `go vet ./...`
- `go test -race ./...`
- module tests
- core coverage: `90.4%`
- Apple VZ provider coverage: `73.2%`
- 25 consecutive race-test runs for helper and provider packages
- 50 consecutive race-test runs for reverse-channel startup, saturation, and recovery
- Windows amd64 and Linux arm64 cross-builds
- 122 script tests
- docs command-surface, link, and site-build checks
- GoReleaser snapshot build
- native helper deployment target verified as macOS 13 with `vtool`
- autoreview clean on final head after all accepted findings

Earlier exact packaged artifact:

- Commit: `90a3b9a68a751b7b572e2764fb3b55f119aa2e3d`
- Version: `0.28.0-SNAPSHOT-90a3b9a6`
- Darwin ARM64 archive SHA-256: `c3838c5615f8a7441936fecc37bd1da84202594032ae91ef3b1e2ed53f6d2648`

Physical Apple Silicon live E2E:

- macOS 26.5 arm64, `kern.hv_support=1`
- final head `9651731dbbe490be0d03147321e8849adf72abae`, lease `cbx_fbc53d7a8a8b`
- created Ubuntu ARM64 VM, reached SSH readiness with systemd running, and verified the root-owned reverse VSOCK proxy service
- restarted the guest proxy service while its 32-channel pool was populated; the next command reused the lease successfully in 348 ms with the new service PID
- deleted the lease and verified an empty Apple VZ inventory
- earlier full lifecycle lease `cbx_54106a4c2316` synced 749 repository files, verified current-main source visibility, ran the command, auto-deleted, and returned an empty provider list
- separate serial-console cap proof wrote 10 MiB from the guest, capped the host log at exactly 8 MiB with a truncation marker, reused the VM, and deleted it
- verified private state/cache permissions and managed-helper codesign entitlements

Clean Parallels macOS guest:

- downloaded and checksum-verified the same archive
- verified native arm64 binaries, embedded version, managed-helper digest, signing, entitlements, and permissions
- doctor reached the expected nested-virtualization boundary only: Parallels reported `kern.hv_support=0`

## Scope

- Apple Silicon macOS 13+
- Linux ARM64 guests
- local direct provider; no broker or cloud account
- no desktop, browser, VNC, Tailscale, checkpoint, fork, or restore surface
target.author: coygeek

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
